title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
High-Risk Investment
Investing your savings can help you earn returns and let your savings grow. When it comes to investments, you can categorise the different types of investment options available based on their risk profile. Some investment avenues are risky, some have low risk, and some have a moderate risk profile.
If you have a healthy risk appetite and do not mind investment risks, you can choose high-risk investments. Let’s understand what these are.
What is a High-Risk Investment?
A high-risk investment is one which puts your savings at a higher risk but also has the potential to deliver higher returns. With high-risk investments, the returns are not guaranteed. They depend on market movements. If the market performs well, high-risk investments can generate attractive returns. However, if the markets do not perform well, you might incur considerable losses.
Features of High-Risk Investments
Some of the features of high-risk investments are as follows –
Market Fluctuations
High-risk return investments are prone to market fluctuations, which can turn volatile. If the market fluctuates considerably, high-risk investments can lead to losses.
High-profit Opportunities
High-risk investments come with the potential of higher returns, too. If the markets perform well, you can generate exponential returns, even within a short time period.
Uncertainty
The returns from high-risk investments are uncertain. They depend on market movements, which are unpredictable and volatile.
Illiquidity
In some instances of high-risk investments, like providing venture capital, liquidity can be an issue. You might not be able to sell your investment and convert it into cash easily. Moreover, in other instances, like stocks, illiquidity can mean the inability to sell your investments without incurring considerable losses. In other words, even if you are able to sell your investment, doing so would result in a loss which you might not want to take. Hence, selling does not seem feasible, and the asset becomes illiquid.
Larger Investment Requirements
Though not true for all high-risk investments, in some cases, you might need a larger investment corpus. Common examples include venture capital, Portfolio Investment Schemes (PIS), investing in start-ups, etc.
Types of High-Risk High Return Investments
There are different options under the high-risk category. However, some of the common ones include the following -
Stocks and Equities
Stocks and equities include equity shares, equity mutual funds, equity funds in ULIPs, private equity, etc. These instruments are linked to the performance of the equity market.
Cryptocurrencies
Cryptocurrencies are types of virtual or digital currencies that use blockchain technology for investment and transactions. Some of the common types of cryptocurrencies include Bitcoin, Ethereum, Ether, etc.
Startups and Venture Capital
You can invest in budding businesses that have the potential to become profitable in the long run. You can fund start-ups or invest in the form of venture capital in different companies.
Initial Public Offerings (IPOs)
IPOs have become a popular option for risk-taking investors. IPOs are launched when a company offers its shares to the public for the very first time. Through IPOs, you can get the stocks of companies being listed for the first time. If the company grows, the share prices rise, and you can make a profit on your investment.
Benefits of High-Risk, High-Return Investments
Some of the benefits of high-risk investments are as follows -
Potential for Significant Gains
Since high-risk investments come with a high-return potential, you can earn attractive returns on your investments if you choose the right avenues and the market performs well.
Diversification
You can add high-risk investments to your portfolio to give it exposure to high-return possibilities. These avenues can balance out high debt exposure and enhance the return potential of your portfolio.
Market Outperformance
In some instances, high-risk investments can even outperform the market. For instance, many IPOs have the capacity to outperform their listing gains when the company performs well.
Early Access to Emerging Markets
With some high-risk investments, like IPOs, venture capital, etc., you can get early access to emerging businesses. If the business grows, so would your profit.
Tips to Invest in High-Risk Investments
When investing in high-risk investments and savings plans, here are some tips that can help -
Proper Research and Checking
Since these investments are risky, it is always better to research and check the investment you are considering. You can also take the help of professional investment advisors to choose the right avenue.
Spreading Your Investments
Diversifying can help in reducing the risks inherent in these investment avenues. Try to invest your savings in different types of investment options to spread the risks.
Understanding Risk and Goals
Before investing in high-risk investments, understand your risk appetite and choose these avenues only if you have the ability to bear the potential losses. Also, align your investments with your goals so that you can create the corpus needed to fund such goals.
Track Performance and Make Adjustments
Track the performance of your investments regularly. If any avenue is not performing as expected, you can redeem it to cut your losses and invest in other avenues that are doing well.
Stay Calm and Stick to Your Plan
Short-term volatility and market fluctuations are common aspects of high-risk investments. It is better to stay calm during uncertain times and stick to your investments. As the market recovers, your investments might start delivering returns again.
Protecting Against Potential Losses
There might be losses with high-risk investments. Hedge against such losses and stay protected. You can invest in debt instruments to minimise the risk exposure of your portfolio. Similarly, gold can be a good tool to hedge against market risks as it has a negative correlation to stocks and other financial instruments.
Key Takeaways
High-risk investments can put your savings at considerable risk, but they have a high return potential too.
Salient features of high-risk investments include exposure to market fluctuations, uncertain returns, illiquidity, etc.
Common investment options that are considered high risk include equity, IPOs, venture capital, cryptocurrency, etc.
When investing in high-risk avenues, it is feasible to do your research, ensure that you have the risk appetite, match the investment to your goals, and make an informed choice.
Conclusion
High-risk investments often appeal to investors for their profit potential. However, when considering these investments, it is better to align your risk profile with them so that you can bear the losses if they arise. Portfolio diversification is also recommended so that you do not depend only on high-risk investments to create a corpus for your goals. Combining different types of assets can help in spreading out investment risks and stabilise your portfolio. So, do your research and choose right so that you can put your savings in the right investment avenues.
FAQs
How can I diversify my portfolio with high-risk investments?
You can add debt instruments to your portfolio to balance out the risk associated with high-risk investments. You can also choose different types of high-risk investments to avoid putting all your eggs in one basket.
Are high-risk investments worth it for long-term growth?
If you have a long-term investment horizon, high-risk investments can prove suitable because market volatility tends to smooth out over time. As such, high-risk investments can help in delivering positive returns on your investments.
What investment is the highest risk?
Every investment has a different degree of risk, and any one avenue cannot be considered to have the highest risk profile. You can compare the risk associated with different investment options to assess which avenue has a higher risk compared to others.
Disclaimer
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.
full_pdf_text:
High-Risk Investment
Investing your savings can help you earn returns and let your savings grow. When it comes to investments, you can categorise the different types of investment options available based on their risk profile. Some investment avenues are risky, some have low risk, and some have a moderate risk profile.
If you have a healthy risk appetite and do not mind investment risks, you can choose high-risk investments. Let’s understand what these are.
What is a High-Risk Investment?
A high-risk investment is one which puts your savings at a higher risk but also has the potential to deliver higher returns. With high-risk investments, the returns are not guaranteed. They depend on market movements. If the market performs well, high-risk investments can generate attractive returns. However, if the markets do not perform well, you might incur considerable losses.
Features of High-Risk Investments
Some of the features of high-risk investments are as follows –
1. Market Fluctuations
High-risk return investments are prone to market fluctuations, which can turn volatile. If the market fluctuates considerably, high-risk investments can lead to losses.
2. High-profit Opportunities
High-risk investments come with the potential of higher returns, too. If the markets perform well, you can generate exponential returns, even within a short time period. 3. Uncertainty
The returns from high-risk investments are uncertain. They depend on market movements, which are unpredictable and volatile. 4. Not very liquid
In some instances of high-risk investments, like providing venture capital, liquidity can be an issue. You might not be able to sell your investment and convert it into cash easily. Moreover, in some cases, such as certain stocks, liquidity risk may arise when trading volumes are low, making it difficult to sell quickly or without accepting a price that is lower than expected. In other words, even if you are able to sell your investment, doing so would result in a loss which you might not want to take. Hence, selling does not seem feasible, and the asset becomes illiquid. 5. Larger Investment Requirements
Though not true for all high-risk investments, in some cases, you might need a larger investment corpus. Common examples include venture capital, Portfolio Investment Schemes (PIS), investing in start-ups, etc.
Types of High-Risk High Return Investments
There are different options under the high-risk category. However, some of the common ones include the following -
1. Stocks and Equities
Stocks and equities include instruments such as equity shares, equity mutual funds, private equity, etc. All these instruments are market‑linked and their performance depends on the movement of the equity markets. 2. Cryptocurrencies
Cryptocurrencies are types of virtual or digital currencies that use blockchain technology for investment and transactions. Some of the common types of cryptocurrencies include Bitcoin, Ethereum, Ether, etc. 3. Startups and Venture Capital
You can support budding businesses that have the potential to grow and become profitable in the long run. 4. Initial Public Offerings (IPOs)
IPOs have become a popular option for risk-taking investors. IPOs are launched when a company offers its shares to the public for the very first time. Through IPOs, you receive shares of a company being listed on the stock exchange for the first time.. If the company grows, the share prices rise, and you can make a profit on your investment.
Benefits of High-Risk, High-Return Investments
Some of the benefits of high-risk investments are as follows -
1. Potential for Significant Gains
Since high-risk investments come with a high-return potential, you can earn attractive returns on your investments if you choose the right avenues and the market performs well. 2. Market Outperformance
In some instances, high-risk investments can even outperform the market. For instance, many IPOs have the capacity to outperform their listing gains when the company performs well. 3. Early Access to Emerging Markets
With some high-risk investments, like IPOs, venture capital, etc., you can get early access to emerging businesses. If the business grows, so would your profit.
Tips to Invest in High-Risk Investments
When investing in high-risk investments and savings plans, here are some tips that can help -
1. Proper Research and Checking
Since these investments are risky, it is always better to research and check the investment you are considering. You can also take the help of professional investment advisors to choose the right avenue. 2. Spreading Your Investments
Diversifying can help in reducing the risks inherent in these investment avenues. Try to invest your savings in different types of investment options to spread the risks. 3. Understanding Risk and Goals
Before investing in high-risk investments, understand your risk appetite and choose these avenues only if you have the ability to bear the potential losses. Also, align your investments with your goals so that you can create the corpus needed to fund such goals. 4. Track Performance and Make Adjustments
Track the performance of your investments regularly. If any avenue is not performing as expected, you can redeem it to cut your losses and invest in other avenues that are doing well. 5. Stay Calm and Stick to Your Plan
Short-term volatility and market fluctuations are common aspects of high-risk investments. It is better to stay calm during uncertain times and stick to your investments. As the market recovers, your investments might start delivering returns again. 6. Protecting Against Potential Losses
High‑risk investments can experience losses due to market fluctuations. To reduce the impact of such losses, you can balance your portfolio with lower‑risk options such as debt instruments. Including a mix of asset types helps spread risk and offers more stability during periods of market volatility. . Similarly, gold can be a good tool to hedge against market risks and it can help you create a fund for your goals.
Key Takeaways
1. High-risk investments can put your savings at considerable risk, but they have a high return
potential too. 2. Salient features of high-risk investments include exposure to market fluctuations, uncertain
returns, illiquidity, etc. 3. Common investment options that are considered high risk include equity, IPOs, venture
capital, cryptocurrency, etc. 4. When investing in high-risk avenues, it is feasible to do your research, ensure that you have
the risk appetite, match the investment to your goals, and make an informed choice.
Conclusion
High-risk investments often appeal to investors for their profit potential. However, when considering these investments, it is better to align your risk profile with them so that you can bear the losses if
they arise. Portfolio diversification is also recommended so that you do not depend only on high-risk investments to create a corpus for your goals. Combining different types of assets can help in spreading out investment risks and stabilise your portfolio. So, do your research and choose right so that you can put your savings in the right investment avenues.
FAQs
1. How can I diversify my portfolio with high-risk investments?
You can diversify your portfolio by combining high‑risk assets with low‑risk instruments, such as debt products, to balance overall risk. You can also spread your exposure across different categories of high‑risk assets instead of concentrating all your money in a single type to avoid putting all your eggs in one basket.
2. Are high-risk investments worth it for long-term growth?
For investors with a long‑term horizon, higher‑risk investments such as equities can potentially deliver higher returns because markets have historically rewarded long‑term risk‑taking. However, returns are not guaranteed, and such investments can experience significant short‑term and long‑term volatility
3. What investment is the highest risk?
Every investment has a different degree of risk, and any one avenue cannot be considered to have the highest risk profile. You can compare the risk associated with different investment options to assess which avenue has a higher risk compared to others.
Disclaimer
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.
